title: Market thread 0 merged
wikitext:
[]: How would I go about ordering things from Evastore.jp? Post by SAWgunner » Tue Feb 03, 2009 1:56 pm Besides the considerable language barrier, what other things would I have to contend with when ordering from there? Will my credit card payment be converted to Yen? Will shipping be a problem (IE extraordinarily expensive or really slow)? Do those kickin' t-shirts really cost $50? I really want some Eva T's that don't make me obviously look like a fanboy but I can't decide if it's worth the cost or not.

[]: Post by Ornette » Tue Feb 03, 2009 3:18 pm You need to buy via a proxy. If you know someone there and they're willing to deal with shipping it from their place to where you are, then talk to them. Otherwise there are some services, although I've never used one.

[]: Post by SAWgunner » Tue Feb 03, 2009 4:01 pm Wow, so they won't even ship it to the States? Oh well, there goes that dream.

[]: Post by BrikHaus » Tue Feb 03, 2009 7:53 pm There are proxy buyers out there. I've used them before, and, at least in my experience, don't charge an unreasonable amount for the service. If there's something from the Eva Store that you really, really want, then you should consider it.

[]: Post by SAWgunner » Tue Feb 03, 2009 8:10 pm How do you find one? I feel a strong desire for a Rei Ayanami "Mass Produced Human" shirt.

[]: Post by cat42 » Fri Feb 06, 2009 11:21 am Just so you know, Amazon.co.jp has some of the Evastore products (not all though) and they ship internationally (or at least to America) for some though, not all.

[]: Post by SAWgunner » Fri Feb 06, 2009 11:41 am They don't seem to have any kickin' Eva hipster shirts. Then again, I can't speak a lick of Japanese so for all I know there's a link to it on the first page. Thanks for the help though.

[]: Post by cat42 » Fri Feb 06, 2009 4:45 pm They have several of them , sadly though, they don't seem to ship those outside of Japan. I bet it's merchandising rights or something. I think they'll only ship DVDs, books, and CDs internationally, however. Damn, and I'd thought I'd found a loophole.

[]: Post by Reichu » Fri Feb 06, 2009 5:09 pm SAWgunner wrote: How do you find one?

[]: Post by SAWgunner » Sat Feb 07, 2009 12:03 am Damn, proxy it is then. Could anyone make any suggestions on a good one? And just to blow off some steam, why the hell won't they ship to America? "Hey there's people over there who want to give us money for stuff that we have. Fuck them! They don't deserve to give us their money!" Is international shipping that much of a pain or are they just making goofy business decisions?

[]: Post by IrkenEvangelion » Sat Feb 07, 2009 12:07 am Because Japan hates us. I've been looking into proxies too and I would also like to know of a good one.

[]: Post by Ornette » Sat Feb 07, 2009 12:38 am SAWgunner wrote: Is international shipping that much of a pain or are they just making goofy business decisions?

[]: Post by SAWgunner » Sat Feb 07, 2009 12:48 am Alright, I figured as much. Doesn't make it any less annoying though.

[]: Post by Reichu » Sat Feb 07, 2009 7:42 pm The last (and only) time I used a proxy was when I splurged on a bunch of Yahoo! JP auctions years ago. It was a small company, and they were very reasonable with prices and shipping things together, but I'd have to relocate and dig through some ancient records to get their name again... And they might not even be around now, or the best bang for your buck. But who needs recommendations from HERE, anyway? You could wait forever for that shit, and this is something you can do yourself, right now (!!!). Like I said, try some Google searches, and you'll turn up results listing both the proxies and people's takes on them. (The Internet is nifty that way.) Here's a broad, messy search to get you started . Have fun...

[]: where can i buy the EVA video games? Post by i_Love_Asuka » Mon Jul 06, 2009 3:52 am hey everybody, i was planning to buy some of the various EVA games for my ancient PS2 but where do you buy them when your outside of Japan? i found the games of my desire at Amazon.Jp but they refuse to sell outside of Japan, so has anyone an adress where i can order them, an importer or something? it schould be shipped to Germany or Belgium, can anyone help? thanks a lot!

[]: Post by Evafan » Mon Jul 06, 2009 2:45 pm I'm not a customer myself, but play-asia.com is supposed to be a good site, or so I'm told.

[]: Post by i_Love_Asuka » Mon Jul 06, 2009 3:53 pm Evafan wrote: I'm not a customer myself, but play-asia.com is supposed to be a good site, or so I'm told.

[]: Post by scarmullet » Mon Jul 06, 2009 4:52 pm i_Love_Asuka wrote: Evafan wrote: I'm not a customer myself, but play-asia.com is supposed to be a good site, or so I'm told.

[]: Post by Jinroh » Mon Jul 06, 2009 5:27 pm EVA 64 is kinda awful. You should definitely try Evangelions 2.

[]: Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Jul 06, 2009 5:52 pm Evangelion 2 is for PSP and PS2, and the nice thing about the PSP port is it's region free. Also, Play Asia is a good site, I usually order Eva games from them.
